intro: Erigeron pyrifolius, also known as the Birch Fleabane, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron pyrifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Birch Fleabane include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation.
